title: Best VPN for India 2026: Top Picks for Privacy, Streaming and Speed
description: The best VPN for India in 2026: NordVPN for speed, ExpressVPN for streaming, Mullvad for privacy. India-specific tips on ISP throttling, censorship, DPDP Act, and JioCinema access.
content:
The best VPN for India in 2026 is NordVPN for most users, ExpressVPN for accessing Indian streaming content from abroad, and Mullvad for users who want the strongest privacy protections. India's internet environment has changed significantly in recent years: government-mandated site blocks, ISP throttling on video content, the new Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP 2023), and recurring regional internet shutdowns make a VPN a practical tool rather than a luxury.

Why You Need a VPN in India

India has specific internet conditions that make a VPN genuinely useful for everyday users, not just privacy enthusiasts.

Government website blocks: India's Department of Telecommunications (DoT) and courts regularly order ISPs to block websites. Torrent sites, certain news platforms, and apps have faced blocks at various times. In 2022, several VPN providers themselves were briefly targeted by CERT-In's logging mandate. A VPN routes your traffic through an encrypted tunnel that your ISP cannot inspect, bypassing block lists at the ISP level.

ISP throttling on video: Reliance Jio, Airtel, and Vi (Vodafone Idea) have been documented throttling streaming video during peak hours, particularly on YouTube and OTT platforms not in their own ecosystem. A VPN prevents your ISP from identifying video traffic, which means it cannot apply differential throttling. Many Indian users report measurably faster streaming with a VPN connected.

The DPDP Act 2023: India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act creates a data principal consent framework, but also grants the government broad exemptions to access personal data for national security. A VPN encrypts your traffic at the device level before it enters the network, limiting what any entity on the network path can observe. The VPN provider's jurisdiction and no-logs policy determine what the provider itself can hand over.

Regional internet shutdowns: India leads the world in government-ordered internet shutdowns. Jammu and Kashmir, Manipur, Rajasthan, and other states have experienced shutdowns ranging from hours to months. While a full shutdown blocks all connectivity, partial throttling and social media blocking during unrest can be circumvented with a VPN. During partial restrictions, VPN traffic is often not included in block orders.

Accessing Indian content from abroad: JioCinema, Hotstar (Disney+ Hotstar), SonyLIV, and ZEE5 require an Indian IP address for certain content, including IPL cricket, Indian Premier League matches, and locally licensed films and series. Indian users living abroad use VPNs to connect to Indian servers and access home content.

Best VPN Providers for India (2026)

1. NordVPN (Best Overall)

NordVPN has servers in India (Mumbai and Chennai) and offers fast connections via the NordLynx protocol, which is based on WireGuard. For users in India connecting to Indian servers, latency is typically under 20 milliseconds on fiber connections. For users abroad connecting to Indian servers, NordVPN's Mumbai servers offer the most consistent speeds of any major provider.

NordVPN holds independent no-logs audits from PricewaterhouseCoopers (2023) and Deloitte (2024). Following CERT-In's 2022 mandate requiring VPN providers to log user data and submit it on request, NordVPN removed its physical servers from India and switched to RAM-only virtual servers. This means no data is written to a physical disk in India and no Indian law enforcement agency can seize physical server hardware to extract logs.

NordVPN works with Netflix India, JioCinema (for India-IP-required content from abroad), and Hotstar. Threat Protection blocks ads and trackers at the DNS level. Price: from 3.09 USD per month on a two-year plan. Up to ten simultaneous device connections. Best choice for most Indian users and for the Indian diaspora accessing home content from abroad.

2. ExpressVPN (Best for Indian Streaming)

ExpressVPN has virtual server locations covering India and is the most consistent performer for accessing JioCinema and Disney+ Hotstar from outside India. Its MediaStreamer Smart DNS service lets you stream Indian content on Smart TVs and streaming sticks that cannot run a VPN app natively.

ExpressVPN also removed its physical Indian servers following CERT-In's logging mandate and operates virtual servers instead. It uses its Lightway protocol (similar to WireGuard but proprietary) and averages 150 to 250 Mbps on international connections. More expensive at 6.67 USD per month on an annual plan. Worth the extra cost if IPL or Hotstar access from abroad is the main use case, as ExpressVPN stays ahead of geo-block detection more consistently than cheaper alternatives.

3. Mullvad (Best for Privacy)

Mullvad operates with no email address at signup, account numbers only, and accepts cash and Monero as payment. In 2023, Swedish police raided Mullvad's offices and left with nothing because Mullvad had no user logs to hand over. This is the gold standard of a no-logs policy proven in practice rather than just claimed in a privacy policy.

Mullvad does not have India-based servers, which means it is not suitable for accessing Indian streaming content from outside India. However, for Indian users who want maximum privacy from their ISP, government surveillance, and CERT-In's data-sharing framework, Mullvad's WireGuard plus DAITA (Defence Against AI-guided Traffic Analysis) implementation is the strongest option available. Price: 5 EUR per month flat. Best for journalists, activists, lawyers, and anyone whose threat model includes state-level surveillance.

4. Surfshark (Best Budget Option for Indian Users)

Surfshark offers India servers (Mumbai) and has no device connection limit, making it ideal for households where multiple people use the same subscription. It removed physical Indian servers after the CERT-In mandate and operates virtual India servers with the same no-logs commitment. Price: 2.19 USD per month on a two-year plan.

Surfshark's Nexus feature routes traffic through a network of servers rather than a single server, which can improve anonymity. CleanWeb blocks ads and phishing domains. Speeds average 120 to 180 Mbps on Indian servers. Good for families and shared households where the per-connection limit on other providers becomes a constraint.

5. Proton VPN (Best Free Option + Swiss Privacy)

Proton VPN is based in Switzerland, outside the jurisdictions of India's DPDP Act and CERT-In's data-sharing requirements. Its free tier has no data cap, no speed throttling on free servers, and a verified no-logs policy. The free tier does not include Indian servers, but paid plans (from 4 EUR per month) do.

Proton's Stealth protocol disguises VPN traffic as regular HTTPS traffic, which helps in environments where deep packet inspection (DPI) is used to block VPN protocols. This is particularly relevant in India, where some ISPs have been documented using DPI to throttle or block VPN connections during sensitive periods. Proton has published transparency reports and a Warrant Canary since 2016.

CERT-In VPN Mandate: What It Means for Users

In April 2022, India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) issued Directions requiring VPN providers to collect and store user data for five years, including names, email addresses, IP addresses, and timestamps. Providers that could not comply due to their no-logs architectures faced the choice of installing logging infrastructure or leaving India.

Most major privacy-focused VPN providers including NordVPN, ExpressVPN, Surfshark, and Proton VPN removed their physical servers from India rather than comply. They now offer virtual Indian servers, which assign users an Indian IP address but route through physical servers outside India (typically in Singapore or the UK). The result: you get an Indian IP address for geo-restricted content, but the actual server hardware sits in a jurisdiction that cannot be compelled by CERT-In.

What this means for you: using any of the major providers listed above, your traffic does not touch Indian server hardware. Your ISP in India can see that you connected to a VPN server, but cannot see your traffic content or destination. The VPN provider's no-logs policy, audited by third parties, determines whether the provider itself has anything to hand over if compelled.

How to Access Indian Streaming Services from Abroad

If you are part of the Indian diaspora in the US, UK, Canada, UAE, or Australia, here is how to access the major Indian streaming platforms:

JioCinema

JioCinema carries IPL cricket, Bollywood films, and Reliance-licensed international content. From outside India, you need an Indian IP address. Connect to a VPN India server, then open JioCinema. NordVPN and ExpressVPN are the most reliable options as of July 2026. Note that JioCinema's app uses geo-IP checks on connection, so you must have the VPN connected before opening the app, not after. If a server gives an error, switch to a different India server in the app.

Disney+ Hotstar

Disney+ Hotstar has separate apps and content libraries for India versus international markets. Indian Hotstar carries IPL, live sports, and Indian-specific licensed content not on Disney+ global. Connect to an India VPN server. ExpressVPN is particularly reliable here because it has MediaStreamer Smart DNS for devices that cannot run a VPN app.

SonyLIV

SonyLIV carries India-licensed sports (ICC cricket, Indian Premier League backup feeds), Sony-original Indian dramas, and licensed international content. It geo-blocks non-Indian IPs. Connect to an India VPN server before opening SonyLIV. NordVPN and Surfshark both work with SonyLIV reliably.

ZEE5

ZEE5 carries Indian regional language content (Telugu, Tamil, Bengali, Marathi) plus Bollywood films. Available via a VPN India server from any country. Speeds vary; use NordVPN's Mumbai server for the lowest latency if you are accessing from Southeast Asia or the UAE.

Is a VPN Legal in India?

Yes, VPNs are legal in India for personal and business use. There is no law prohibiting VPN software or encrypted connections. The CERT-In mandate applies to VPN service providers operating in India, not to users of VPNs. Using a VPN to browse privately, access geo-restricted content, or secure your connection on public Wi-Fi is lawful.

Using a VPN to access streaming content from India while abroad may violate the streaming platform's terms of service (since Indian subscriptions are priced for the Indian market). This is a contractual issue between you and the platform, not a criminal matter. The platforms can terminate your account, but there is no legal liability for the user.

VPN and ISP Throttling in India

ISP throttling in India is well-documented. Ookla's Speedtest data and independent studies by the Internet Freedom Foundation have shown that Jio, Airtel, and Vi throttle YouTube, Netflix, and other video platforms during peak hours (typically 7 PM to 11 PM). The throttling works by the ISP identifying video streaming traffic via deep packet inspection and applying reduced bandwidth to those connections.

A VPN encrypts all traffic, including video streams. From the ISP's perspective, all VPN traffic looks identical. The ISP cannot distinguish video traffic from any other data inside the encrypted tunnel, so it cannot apply video-specific throttling. Users on throttled connections who connect through a VPN typically see a measurable improvement in streaming quality during peak hours.

Note: this only works if the ISP is selectively throttling video rather than reducing overall bandwidth. If the ISP reduces all traffic including VPN traffic during congestion, the VPN does not help. Selective application-layer throttling is where a VPN is effective.

Free vs Paid VPN for India

Free VPNs are generally inadequate for the specific challenges of using the internet in India:

  • No India servers: Most free VPN tiers do not include Indian servers. For users abroad wanting Indian content access, or for users in India needing privacy-preserving local connectivity, this is a hard limitation.
  • Data caps: Free tiers typically cap at 500 MB to 2 GB per month. Streaming an IPL match uses 2 to 4 GB per hour at HD quality.
  • Protocol limitations: During internet shutdowns or DPI-enforcement periods, free VPNs often use only OpenVPN, which is easier to detect and block than Stealth or Lightway protocols.
  • Privacy risks: Several free VPN apps have been caught logging user data and selling it. In India's current data environment, using a logging VPN defeats the purpose.

Proton VPN Free is the exception: it has no data cap, verified no-logs policy, and Swiss jurisdiction. It is the only free VPN credible for privacy use in India, though its free tier does not include Indian servers. For Indian content access or full-speed connections, a paid plan at 2 to 5 USD per month is a more practical choice.

How to Set Up a VPN in India

Setup takes under five minutes on Android, iOS, Windows, or Mac:

  1. Choose a provider. For most users: NordVPN. For Indian streaming from abroad: ExpressVPN. For maximum privacy: Mullvad.
  2. Download the app from the provider's official website. Do not download from third-party APK sites or unverified app stores.
  3. Create an account and subscribe. Mullvad accepts cash by mail and Monero if you want to sign up without an email.
  4. Open the app. To access Indian content from abroad: connect to an India server (Mumbai recommended for lowest latency from Southeast Asia and UAE). To protect your traffic inside India from ISP throttling or surveillance: connect to a fast international server in Singapore or Netherlands for speed.
  5. Verify the connection by visiting ipleak.net or dnsleaktest.com. Confirm the IP address shown is not your ISP's address.
  6. Enable the kill switch in app settings. This cuts all internet access if the VPN drops, preventing your real IP from leaking mid-session.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best VPN for India in 2026?

NordVPN is the best overall VPN for India in 2026. It has virtual Indian servers in Mumbai and Chennai, a third-party audited no-logs policy, and fast NordLynx speeds. For accessing JioCinema and Hotstar from outside India, ExpressVPN is the most reliable choice.

Are VPN servers in India safe after the CERT-In mandate?

Most major VPN providers removed their physical servers from India after CERT-In's 2022 logging mandate. They now offer virtual Indian servers hosted outside India (typically Singapore or UK), which assign you an Indian IP but are not subject to CERT-In's jurisdiction. NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark all use this approach. Physical Indian servers from providers that complied with the mandate are not private.

Can I watch IPL cricket on JioCinema from outside India?

Yes. Connect to an India VPN server before opening JioCinema. NordVPN and ExpressVPN are the most reliable options for this as of July 2026. The VPN must be connected before you launch the app, not after. If one India server fails, switch to another in the same app.

Is using a VPN legal in India?

Yes, VPNs are fully legal in India for personal and business use. The CERT-In mandate targeted VPN providers, not users. You can use a VPN freely for privacy, streaming, or securing your connection on public Wi-Fi without any legal risk.

Does a VPN help with ISP throttling in India?

Yes, when throttling is applied at the application layer (where the ISP identifies video traffic specifically). A VPN encrypts all traffic equally, preventing your ISP from applying differential throttling to video streams. Most ISP throttling in India targets specific video traffic, so a VPN is effective in these cases.

Which VPN is best for Indian users who want maximum privacy?

Mullvad is the best option for maximum privacy. It requires no email to sign up, accepts cash and Monero, and has proven its no-logs policy when Swedish police raided its offices in 2023 and found no data. Its WireGuard with DAITA implementation resists traffic analysis. Proton VPN is the second-best option for users who want Swiss jurisdiction and a credible free tier.

Does NordVPN have Indian servers?

NordVPN has virtual Indian servers (Mumbai and Chennai) since removing its physical Indian servers after the 2022 CERT-In mandate. These virtual servers assign Indian IP addresses while the physical hardware sits outside India. All NordVPN plans include Indian server access.
